meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Design with AI: Overcome Creative Block (Student Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank IDE. Use this intermediate checklist to design high-impact AI projects with Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project paralysis requires shifting from 'what to build' to 'what problem to solve.' By using Antigravity to architect your system requirements and Claude Code to iterate on the implementation, you transform abstract ideas into production-ready code, effectively building a portfolio that demonstrates both engineering rigor and modern AI proficiency.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor in your IDE isn't just a UI element; it's a wall. You've mastered the syntax, you've completed the tutorials, and you're ready to build something that matters. Yet, every time you sit down to start, the sheer expanse of 'possible' leaves you paralyzed. This is the 'blank page' syndrome, the silent killer of engineering careers.

But the game has changed. We are no longer limited by our own cognitive bandwidth. By leveraging AI-assisted design, you can translate vague market needs into structured technical specifications in seconds. This isn't about letting the machine do the work; it's about shifting your role from a typist to an architect. Let's break through the noise and build your first high-impact project.

steps.title: Define the Friction Point
steps.description: Don't build a 'todo app.' Identify a specific, annoying process in your academic or daily life. Use Antigravity to map the logical flow of a solution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product architect. Help me brainstorm 3 B2B SaaS project ideas that solve student productivity issues, focusing on data extraction and automation. Output a feature list for the most viable idea.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project requirements document for a student-focused task automation tool based on the top priority feature list.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Schema
steps.description: AI-driven apps live or die by their data structure. Define your entities before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a task automation tool. Include tables for users, tasks, and API integrations. Use Prisma schema syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create schema.prisma file and run migration to initialize the database.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core Service
steps.description: Focus on the 'engine' of your app—the AI integration layer—before worrying about the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular Node.js service that handles OpenAI API requests with error handling and rate limiting. Use TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/services/aiService.ts and implement a robust request wrapper with types.'

steps.title: Iterative Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out features incrementally, ensuring each module is tested before moving to the next.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the step-by-step logic to implement a webhook listener for incoming task data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the webhook controller and add unit tests using Jest.'

steps.title: Refinement and Documentation
steps.description: A project is only as good as its documentation. Use your tools to generate a README that sells your engineering process to future employers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this project, highlighting the architectural choices, the tech stack, and the specific problems solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the current codebase and generate a README.md that includes installation instructions and system architecture diagrams.'

pro_tips: Always ask your AI tool 'What are the edge cases?' before finalizing any function logic; it will save you hours of debugging.
Treat your AI session like a pair-programming partner: if the code looks too complex, ask it to refactor for readability first.
Document your failures. If a prompt led to a bad implementation, save that prompt in a 'lessons learned' folder for your portfolio.
Focus on one 'hero' feature. Don't try to build a full platform; build one interaction that works perfectly.
Run tests before asking the AI to optimize code. If it isn't broken, don't let the AI 'improve' it into a bug.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, document the deployment process in your README, and share your repository with a peer for code review.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework, the tools, and the workflow to stop dreaming and start shipping. Don't let this checklist gather dust in your bookmarks. Pick an idea, initialize your project, and execute the first step today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
